name: The Police Credit Union Bryant Street Branch
description_short: Credit union serving law enforcement and their communities with mortgages, auto loans, home improvement loans, and member benefits.
description_long:
The Police Credit Union (SFPCU) is a financial institution specifically designed to serve law enforcement officers and their communities. Founded to address the unique financial needs of the law enforcement sector, the credit union has built a membership base that values competitive rates and personalized service. The organization operates multiple branch locations, including the Bryant Street Branch, and maintains a physical and virtual presence to serve members across different channels.

The Police Credit Union offers a comprehensive range of financial products tailored to law enforcement personnel. Their core offerings include home loans with H.E.R.O. mortgages (0% down for first-time homebuying law enforcement), auto loans starting at 4.94% APR, home improvement loans up to $75,000 at rates as low as 8.24% APR, and a Platinum Visa card with rewards. Specialized products include the 10-Eight Loan for new law enforcement employees, a Green Energy Loan for renewable energy purchases, and an End of Watch debt forgiveness program providing up to $850,000 in coverage.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its mission-focused approach to law enforcement and first-responder communities. They offer member benefits including annual scholarships ($2,500 awards, five per year), special member discounts, and specialized loan products that address unique law enforcement circumstances. The organization provides virtual branch services with extended hours and emphasizes competitive rates across product categories. Customer testimonials highlight satisfaction with service quality and rate competitiveness.

The credit union operates as a standard financial institution with membership requirements and a $25 minimum opening deposit. While their products are competitively positioned, actual rates and terms vary based on credit history and qualification. The organization requires borrowers to be U.S. Citizens or Permanent Residents, and certain products like home improvement loans carry specific property and eligibility restrictions. Like traditional lenders, approval depends on credit evaluation and underwriting standards.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: H.E.R.O. mortgages with 0% down payment for active law enforcement first-time homebuyers
Auto loan rates as low as 4.94% APR with current 1% discount promotion at Enterprise Car Sales
Home improvement loans up to $75,000 with rates starting at 8.24% APR
End of Watch debt forgiveness program providing up to $850,000 coverage for members' loved ones
Five $2,500 annual scholarships awarded to member students for academic achievement and community service
Virtual branch services available Monday-Friday 10am-5pm PST and Saturday 9am-3pm PST
Specialized 10-Eight Loan for new law enforcement employees to fund equipment and supplies
cons: Membership restricted to law enforcement, their families, and specific community members—not open to general public
Home improvement loans limited to one per membership/household and for primary residence only
Maximum vehicle mileage of 175,000 and new auto definition limited to 2025-2026 models with under 7,500 miles
Actual APR varies based on credit history; quoted rates assume 'excellent borrower history' and may be higher
Requires U.S. Citizenship or Permanent Resident status with valid 10-year renewal card
best_for: Law enforcement officers seeking mortgages, auto loans, and home improvement financing with specialized rates and products
First-time homebuying police officers eligible for the H.E.R.O. 0% down mortgage program
New law enforcement employees needing equipment and supply financing through the 10-Eight Loan
Law enforcement families seeking member benefits including scholarships and special discounts
diagnosis: The Police Credit Union is best for law enforcement officers and their families seeking competitive rates on mortgages, auto loans, and specialty products designed for their circumstances. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to law enforcement and affiliated individuals—this is not a general-population credit union—and actual approval rates and terms depend on individual creditworthiness and eligibility requirements.
services: H.E.R.O. Mortgages (0% down for law enforcement first-time homebuyers)
Home loans with fixed and variable rate mortgages
Home Improvement Loans (up to $75,000, rates from 8.24% APR)
Auto loans (rates from 4.94% APR for new vehicles)
Platinum Visa credit card (rates from 12.75% APR with rewards program)
Green Energy Loan (up to $75,000 for renewable energy)
End of Watch Debt Forgiveness Program (up to $850,000 coverage)
Savings and checking accounts (opening deposit $25 minimum)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
